_about: Synthesized /scrutineer-mcp verdicts for the top corpus, applying mcp_risk_guidance.yaml's rubric to the analyzer evidence (Pass 1 config + Pass 2 tool surface; no source review). 'security' is the two-axis verdict. 'data_sensitivity_analyzer' is what the tool's data_profile emitted; 'data_sensitivity_review' is the corrected human rating where the analyzer mis-rated. This file is the regression baseline — re-run run_analysis.sh and diff.

_method_notes.caution_floor: Every entry uses the unpinned `npx -y` / `uvx` form its README publishes => mutable_install_path => capping rule 'mutable install path cannot be SAFE' => CAUTION is the floor for all 22, independent of behavior. Pinning an exact version + lockfile is the single change that lifts the well-behaved ones toward SAFE.
_method_notes.block_rule: Hard-BLOCK trigger 'unpinned remote execution with sensitive env (mutable_install_path + sensitive_env_required)' fires for every credentialed server installed unpinned. As configured per their READMEs, credentialed MCPs BLOCK.
_method_notes.caps_are_candidates: Per-tool capabilities are basis=declared CANDIDATES from name/description/schema keyword matching. The review column corrects the frequent false positives (e.g. network_egress on every filesystem tool, code_execution on read_text_file, privilege_escalation on tavily_crawl).

everything.note: Reference/test server. Unpinned => CAUTION floor. HS rating is a false high driven by the demo tools spanning many data categories; in practice it holds nothing.

filesystem.note: Correct HS — reads any file under the granted root (source, .env, keys). broad_filesystem_scope flagged (pointed at ~/Projects). NOTE: the two HIGH toxic-combos are FALSE — network_egress was mis-tagged on every fs tool; this server makes no network calls.

memory.note: Local knowledge-graph store; persists whatever you write to it. file_delete/network_egress caps are false positives.

sequential-thinking.note: FALSE HIGH. Pure reasoning scratchpad, one tool, no external data access. Mis-rated HS via source_code category match on the 'thought' field.

git.note: Reads/searches repo contents => source_code (critical). Correct. broad_filesystem_scope on the --repository path.

time.note: Timezone conversion only. Cleanest server in the set; CAUTION is purely the unpinned floor.

fetch.note: Fetches arbitrary URLs => SSRF / internal-endpoint reach not modeled by the data tier. Worth a source pass in a real review.

github.note: BLOCK as-configured: unpinned + GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN (mutable install receiving a broad-scope credential). Pin + scope the token to lift to CAUTION.

slack.note: BLOCK: unpinned + SLACK_BOT_TOKEN. HS correct — 4 tools read message/DM contents (critical tier). (Capability scan tagged 0 caps — a miss — but data_profile caught the content access.)

puppeteer.note: Headless browser control; can reach authenticated pages. code_execution cap is plausible here (script evaluation) unlike the read-tool false positives.

postgres.note: UNDER-rated. Tool surface is just 'query', so the analyzer sees MINIMAL — but the actual data tier is whatever lives in the database (often PII/financial). Data sensitivity should be assessed against the target DB, not the tool name.

brave-search.note: BLOCK: unpinned + BRAVE_API_KEY. database_access cap is a false positive (search != DB). Contrast brave-search-official below — same service, HS rating: a data-tier instability.

playwright.note: Only server pinned via @latest (pin_strength=latest, still mutable => CAUTION). Browser automation; SENSITIVE is slightly high absent stored creds.

context7.note: BLOCK: unpinned + CONTEXT7_API_KEY (key is optional in practice). Docs lookup; genuinely low sensitivity.

exa.note: BLOCK: unpinned + EXA_API_KEY. Web search; low sensitivity.

notion.note: BLOCK: unpinned + NOTION_TOKEN. HS correct — full workspace read/write.

stripe.note: Server would not boot without a real STRIPE_SECRET_KEY (initialize closed stdout) => no tool surface captured, so analyzer rated MINIMAL. True exposure is financial (critical). BLOCK: unpinned + secret key. Illustrates that 'no tools captured' must NOT read as 'low sensitivity'.

firecrawl.note: BLOCK: unpinned + FIRECRAWL_API_KEY. code_execution (browser_execute) is plausible; file_delete on firecrawl_search is a false positive.

tavily.note: BLOCK: unpinned + TAVILY_API_KEY. privilege_escalation on tavily_crawl/tavily_map is a clear false positive.

brave-search-official.note: BLOCK: unpinned + BRAVE_API_KEY. HS is high vs the MINIMAL given to the other brave server — same vendor, 8 tools incl. local/location search. Data-tier rating is unstable across near-identical services.

sentry.note: CAUTION not BLOCK only because the token is passed as a CLI ARG (--access-token=) not env, so sensitive_env_required didn't fire — yet a secret on argv is arguably worse (process-list leak). HS correct (errors/stack traces/source snapshots). code_execution + privilege_escalation caps are false positives.
